questions.question: Explain the objectives of crop variety improvement.
questions.answer: Crop variety improvement aims to increase crop yield, improve quality, develop resistance to diseases and pests and enable crops to tolerate adverse environmental conditions.

questions.question: Explain the classification of crops based on seasons.
questions.answer: Crops are classified into Kharif crops grown during the rainy season and Rabi crops grown during the winter season.

questions.question: Explain nutrient management in crop production.
questions.answer: Nutrient management involves supplying essential nutrients to crops through manure and fertilizers to ensure proper plant growth and development.

questions.question: Explain the advantages and disadvantages of fertilizers.
questions.answer: Fertilizers provide nutrients quickly and increase crop yield but excessive use can harm soil fertility and pollute water bodies.

questions.question: Explain the advantages of manure in agriculture.
questions.answer: Manure improves soil fertility, increases water holding capacity and enhances soil structure.

questions.question: Explain the importance of irrigation in crop production.
questions.answer: Irrigation supplies water to crops during dry periods and helps maintain soil moisture for proper plant growth.

questions.question: Explain mixed cropping and its advantages.
questions.answer: Mixed cropping is growing two or more crops together on the same land to reduce risk of crop failure.

questions.question: Explain intercropping with examples.
questions.answer: Intercropping is growing two or more crops in definite rows such as maize and beans.

questions.question: Explain crop rotation and its importance.
questions.answer: Crop rotation involves growing different crops in succession to maintain soil fertility and reduce pests.

questions.question: Explain crop protection management.
questions.answer: Crop protection management includes methods to protect crops from pests, weeds and diseases.

questions.question: Explain methods used for weed control.
questions.answer: Weeds can be controlled by manual removal, mechanical methods and chemical herbicides.

questions.question: Explain the importance of animal husbandry.
questions.answer: Animal husbandry improves production of milk, meat, eggs and other animal products.

questions.question: Explain cattle farming and its benefits.
questions.answer: Cattle farming provides milk and helps in agricultural work.

questions.question: Explain poultry farming and its importance.
questions.answer: Poultry farming provides eggs and meat which are rich sources of protein.

questions.question: Explain fish production methods.
questions.answer: Fish production includes capture fishing and culture fishing.

questions.question: Explain aquaculture and its importance.
questions.answer: Aquaculture is the farming of fish and other aquatic organisms to increase fish production.

questions.question: Explain composite fish culture.
questions.answer: Composite fish culture involves growing different fish species together to utilize food resources efficiently.

questions.question: Explain bee-keeping and its benefits.
questions.answer: Bee-keeping produces honey and wax and improves pollination.

questions.question: Explain pasturage in apiculture.
questions.answer: Pasturage refers to the availability of flowers that provide nectar for bees.

questions.question: Explain the importance of fish as a food source.
questions.answer: Fish is rich in protein and nutrients and provides inexpensive food.

questions.question: Explain the importance of grain storage.
questions.answer: Grain storage protects harvested crops from pests, moisture and microorganisms.

questions.question: Explain the causes of grain spoilage.
questions.answer: Grain spoilage occurs due to insects, rodents, moisture and microorganisms.

questions.question: Explain balanced feed for cattle.
questions.answer: Balanced feed contains roughage and concentrates to maintain animal health.

questions.question: Explain integrated farming.
questions.answer: Integrated farming combines crop production with livestock and fish farming to improve productivity.

questions.question: Explain sustainable agriculture.
questions.answer: Sustainable agriculture maintains soil fertility and reduces environmental damage while ensuring food production.
